proposed_hypotheses.text: The dataset contains a binary outcome `objective_response` whose marginal rate is non-trivial (between 10% and 50%) and whose distribution differs across the six treatment indicators.
All patients in this prostate-cancer cohort are male, so `sex_female` is constant and uninformative for any modeling.

analyses.code: df['objective_response'].mean(); df.groupby(t)['objective_response'].mean() for t in treatments
analyses.result_summary: Overall objective_response rate = 0.2402 (n=50000). Response rates by treatment: enzalutamide 0.3614 (n=20076), abiraterone 0.2381 (n=14991), docetaxel 0.2399 (n=15187), olaparib 0.2393 (n=5098), lu177-PSMA 0.2427 (n=7504), pembrolizumab 0.2387 (n=2384). Only enzalutamide deviates clearly from the marginal.

analyses.code: df['sex_female'].unique()
analyses.result_summary: sex_female has a single unique value (0). All 50,000 patients are male; the variable is uninformative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_enzalutamide` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients not receiving it.

Patients receiving `treatment_abiraterone`, `treatment_docetaxel`, `treatment_olaparib`, `treatment_lu177_psma`, or `treatment_pembrolizumab` do not have meaningfully different unadjusted response rates than patients not receiving each respective drug.

analyses.code: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['treatment_enzalutamide'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide-treated: response 0.3614 vs untreated 0.1589; absolute difference +0.2024 (chi-square p ≈ 0).

analyses.code: for tx in [abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, pembrolizumab]: chi2 on tx vs response
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted response differences (treated minus untreated): abiraterone -0.0030 (p=0.47), docetaxel -0.0005 (p=0.91), olaparib -0.0010 (p=0.89), lu177-PSMA +0.0029 (p=0.60), pembrolizumab -0.0016 (p=0.88). None are significant or clinically meaningful.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `mcrpc` = 1 (castration-resistant disease) have a substantially lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `mcrpc` = 0 (hormone-sensitive disease).
Higher `ecog_ps` is associated with lower `objective_response`, monotonically across ECOG 0/1/2.
`visceral_mets` and `gleason_score` have negligible main effects on `objective_response`.

analyses.code: df.groupby('mcrpc')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: mcrpc=0 response 0.3460 vs mcrpc=1 response 0.1535; absolute difference -0.1925 (chi-square p ≈ 0). Very large effect.

analyses.code: df.groupby('ecog_ps')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0 response 0.282, ECOG 1 0.228, ECOG 2 0.182. Monotone decline; chi-square p = 5.4e-72.

analyses.code: chi2 for visceral_mets and for gleason_score vs response
analyses.result_summary: visceral_mets: 0.2404 vs 0.2395 (p=0.87). gleason_score 6/7/8/9/10: 0.2435/0.2371/0.2422/0.2417/0.2424 (chi-square p=0.77). Both essentially null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `brca2_mutation` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with brca2_mutation = 0.
Patients with `ar_v7_positive` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than ar_v7_positive = 0 patients.
Patients with `msi_high` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than msi_high = 0 patients.
`psma_high` has no main effect on `objective_response`.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['brca2_mutation'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2-mutated: 0.1497 vs wild-type 0.2503; difference -0.1006 (p = 5.4e-56).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['ar_v7_positive'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: AR-V7 positive: 0.1599 vs negative 0.2604; difference -0.1005 (p = 1.9e-98).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['msi_high'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: MSI-high: 0.1760 vs MSI-low/stable 0.2422; difference -0.0662 (p = 3.0e-9).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['psma_high'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: PSMA-high: 0.2386 vs PSMA-not-high 0.2426; difference -0.0040 (p = 0.305). Null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher baseline `psa_ng_ml` is associated with lower probability of `objective_response`.
Higher baseline `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher probability of `objective_response`.
Higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` and higher `crp_mg_l` are associated with lower probability of `objective_response`.
Standard chemistry/CBC labs (LDH, hemoglobin, ALP, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, Na, K, Ca, NLR) and `age_years` show no main effect on `objective_response`.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of psa_ng_ml in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean PSA: responders 28.24 vs non-responders 47.23 ng/mL (diff -18.99, Welch p=1.5e-137). Strong negative association.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of albumin_g_dl in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean albumin: responders 3.820 vs non-responders 3.796 g/dL (diff +0.024, p=3.6e-6). Small but significant positive association.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of weight_loss_pct_6mo and crp_mg_l in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Weight loss %: responders 3.50 vs non-responders 3.95 (diff -0.45, p=6.8e-31). CRP: responders 5.69 vs non-responders 6.23 mg/L (diff -0.53, p=9.3e-9). Both negative as predicted.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests for each lab and age vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: All p-values >0.13 (LDH p=0.68, Hb p=0.51, ALP p=0.34, AST p=0.22, ALT p=0.51, bilirubin p=0.18, creatinine p=0.72, BUN p=0.72, Na p=0.60, K p=0.13, Ca p=0.80, NLR p=0.61, age p=0.82). No detectable main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features, `treatment_enzalutamide` retains a strong positive odds ratio for `objective_response`, while `treatment_abiraterone`, `treatment_docetaxel`, `treatment_olaparib`, `treatment_lu177_psma`, and `treatment_pembrolizumab` are not associated with response.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[all_features])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted multivariable logistic ORs for response: enzalutamide OR=3.32 (p<1e-300), abiraterone OR=0.98 (p=0.31), docetaxel OR=1.00 (p=0.95), olaparib OR=1.03 (p=0.49), lu177-PSMA OR=1.03 (p=0.35), pembrolizumab OR=1.01 (p=0.79). Only enzalutamide is significant; the other five drugs show no adjusted main effect on response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of `treatment_enzalutamide` on `objective_response` is markedly larger in `mcrpc` = 0 (hormone-sensitive) patients than in `mcrpc` = 1 (castration-resistant) patients.

analyses.code: Stratified rates and logistic interaction enz x mcrpc
analyses.result_summary: Stratified rates: in mcrpc=0, enzalutamide 0.6096 vs 0.1693 (diff +0.4403, p≈0). In mcrpc=1, enzalutamide 0.1581 vs 0.1505 (diff +0.0077, p=0.09). Logistic interaction enz×mcrpc OR=0.128 (beta=-2.05, p≈0); enzalutamide benefit is essentially confined to hormone-sensitive disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of `treatment_enzalutamide` on `objective_response` is abolished in patients with `ar_v7_positive` = 1 (treatment x AR-V7 negative interaction).

analyses.code: Stratify by ar_v7_positive; logistic with enz x ar_v7_positive interaction
analyses.result_summary: AR-V7 negative: enzalutamide 0.4105 vs 0.1595 (diff +0.2510, p≈0). AR-V7 positive: enzalutamide 0.1650 vs 0.1565 (diff +0.0085, p=0.27). Adjusted interaction OR=0.259 (beta=-1.35, p=9e-104). Strong, consistent with AR-V7 conferring resistance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of `treatment_enzalutamide` on `objective_response` is abolished in patients with `brca2_mutation` = 1 (treatment x BRCA2 wild-type interaction).

analyses.code: Stratify by brca2_mutation; logistic with enz x brca2 interaction
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2 wild-type: enzalutamide 0.3848 vs 0.1602 (diff +0.2246, p≈0). BRCA2 mutated: enzalutamide 0.1535 vs 0.1471 (diff +0.0063, p=0.57). Adjusted interaction OR=0.292 (beta=-1.23, p=4e-46).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of `treatment_enzalutamide` on `objective_response` is abolished in patients with `msi_high` = 1 (treatment x MSI-low interaction).

analyses.code: Stratify by msi_high; logistic with enz x msi_high interaction
analyses.result_summary: MSI not high: enzalutamide 0.3673 vs 0.1581 (diff +0.2092, p≈0). MSI-high: enzalutamide 0.1623 vs 0.1844 (diff -0.0221, p=0.30). Adjusted interaction OR=0.247 (beta=-1.40, p=2e-21). MSI-high also nullifies enzalutamide benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Enzalutamide does NOT interact with `psma_high` or `visceral_mets` for objective response (i.e., its benefit is similar in PSMA-high vs not, and in visceral-mets vs not, holding other things equal).

analyses.code: Stratify by psma_high and visceral_mets; logistic interaction tests
analyses.result_summary: psma_high: enz benefit +0.2042 vs +0.1998 (interaction OR 1.04, p=0.37). visceral_mets: enz benefit +0.2030 vs +0.2001 (interaction OR 1.00, p=0.97). Both null - enzalutamide effect does not depend on PSMA expression or visceral metastases.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `mcrpc` = 0, `brca2_mutation` = 0, `ar_v7_positive` = 0, and `msi_high` = 0, `treatment_enzalutamide` produces a very large absolute increase in `objective_response` rate (≥ +0.50).

analyses.code: Subset to mcrpc=0 & brca2=0 & ar_v7=0 & msi=0; chi2 of enzalutamide vs response
analyses.result_summary: Joint favorable subgroup n=15,681. Enzalutamide-treated response 0.7978 (n=6,325) vs untreated 0.1721 (n=9,356); absolute difference +0.6257 (chi-square p ≈ 0). Confirms a treatment-effect concentration of ≈+63 percentage points.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four 'unfavorable' markers individually nullifies enzalutamide benefit: when only `mcrpc` = 1 (other three favorable) the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is essentially zero.
When only `brca2_mutation` = 1 (other three favorable), the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is essentially zero.
When only `ar_v7_positive` = 1 (other three favorable), the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is essentially zero.
When only `msi_high` = 1 (other three favorable), the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is essentially zero.

analyses.code: subset where mcrpc=1 & brca2=0 & arv7=0 & msi=0; compare enz vs no-enz
analyses.result_summary: n=19,163. Enzalutamide 0.1594 vs 0.1520 (diff +0.0074, chi-square p=0.17). Effect abolished.

analyses.code: subset where mcrpc=0 & brca2=1 & arv7=0 & msi=0; compare enz vs no-enz
analyses.result_summary: n=1,775. Enzalutamide 0.1521 vs 0.1484 (diff +0.0038, p=0.88). Effect abolished by BRCA2 mutation alone.

analyses.code: subset where mcrpc=0 & brca2=0 & arv7=1 & msi=0; compare enz vs no-enz
analyses.result_summary: n=3,940. Enzalutamide 0.1825 vs 0.1610 (diff +0.0215, p=0.086). Effect abolished by AR-V7 positivity alone.

analyses.code: subset where mcrpc=0 & brca2=0 & arv7=0 & msi=1; compare enz vs no-enz
analyses.result_summary: n=494. Enzalutamide 0.1852 vs 0.2098 (diff -0.0247, p=0.58). Effect abolished by MSI-high alone.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A simple greedy decision tree trained to identify patients with high enzalutamide CATE (estimated by GBM meta-learner) recovers exactly the rule mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0.

analyses.code: GradientBoosting fit separately on enz=1 and enz=0; CATE = pred_t - pred_c; DecisionTree(max_depth=4) on (CATE>0.30)
analyses.result_summary: Tree's only positive (large CATE) leaf is mcrpc<=0.5 & ar_v7_positive<=0.5 & brca2_mutation<=0.5 & msi_high<=0.5. All other branches yield CATE<0.30. Mean CATE 0.437 in mcrpc=0 vs 0.009 in mcrpc=1. Confirms the four-marker subgroup definition is essentially complete.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A logistic model with all four enz×marker interactions (mcrpc, brca2, ar_v7, msi) plus main effects shows a very large positive enzalutamide main coefficient (in the all-favorable cell) and four large negative interactions, confirming the four-component subgroup formula.

analyses.code: sm.Logit on response, including enz, mcrpc, brca2, arv7, msi, and four interactions
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ORs: enzalutamide main = 13.80 (p≈0); enz x mcrpc = 0.107 (p≈0); enz x ar_v7 = 0.193 (p=6e-140); enz x brca2 = 0.211 (p=4e-67); enz x msi_high = 0.189 (p=2e-27). Each interaction collapses the OR back near 1, matching the all-or-nothing pattern observed in stratified cells.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `treatment_olaparib` does NOT improve `objective_response` in patients with `brca2_mutation` = 1 (no interaction with BRCA2).
`treatment_pembrolizumab` does NOT improve `objective_response` in patients with `msi_high` = 1.
`treatment_lu177_psma` does NOT improve `objective_response` in patients with `psma_high` = 1.

analyses.code: Subset brca2=1; chi2 of olaparib vs response; logistic interaction olaparib x brca2
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2 mutated: olaparib response 0.1210 (n=529) vs no olaparib 0.1531 (n=4,467); diff -0.032 (p=0.058). Logistic interaction olaparib x brca2 OR=0.74 (p=0.05). Trend toward worse, certainly no benefit.

analyses.code: Subset msi_high=1; chi2 of pembrolizumab vs response
analyses.result_summary: MSI-high: pembrolizumab response 0.1772 (n=79) vs no pembrolizumab 0.1760 (n=1,449); diff +0.0012 (Fisher p≈1). Logistic interaction pembro x msi OR=0.96 (p=0.89). No benefit.

analyses.code: Subset psma_high=1; chi2 of lu177_psma vs response
analyses.result_summary: PSMA-high: lu177-PSMA response 0.2376 (n=4,486) vs no lu177-PSMA 0.2388 (n=25,476); diff -0.0011 (p=0.88). Logistic interaction lu177 x psma OR=0.96 (p=0.51). No benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `treatment_abiraterone` does not improve `objective_response` in any of these candidate subgroups: `mcrpc` = 0, `mcrpc` = 1, `ar_v7_positive` = 0, `brca2_mutation` = 0, or in the joint hormone-sensitive favorable subgroup.
`treatment_docetaxel` does not improve `objective_response` in any of the same candidate subgroups.

analyses.code: Stratified abiraterone effect across the listed subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Abiraterone effect (treated-untreated): mcrpc=0 -0.0096 (p=0.17), mcrpc=1 +0.0008 (p=0.89), ar_v7=0 -0.0049 (p=0.31), brca2=0 -0.0054 (p=0.23), favorable subgroup -0.0121 (p=0.16). All non-significant; no subgroup of benefit detected.

analyses.code: Stratified docetaxel effect across the listed subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Docetaxel effect (treated-untreated): mcrpc=0 +0.0026 (p=0.71), mcrpc=1 -0.0015 (p=0.77), ar_v7=0 -0.0007 (p=0.89), brca2=0 +0.0005 (p=0.92), favorable subgroup +0.0054 (p=0.54). Null everywhere.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the enzalutamide-favorable subgroup (`mcrpc`=0 & brca2=0 & ar_v7=0 & msi_high=0), no other treatment (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab) further changes `objective_response`.

analyses.code: fav subset; chi2 of each non-enzalutamide treatment vs response
analyses.result_summary: In favorable subgroup (n=15,681), treated-vs-untreated differences: abiraterone -0.0121 (p=0.16), docetaxel +0.0054 (p=0.54), olaparib -0.0008 (p=0.97), lu177-PSMA -0.0071 (p=0.53), pembrolizumab -0.0168 (p=0.40). Enzalutamide is the sole effective drug.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the enzalutamide-favorable subgroup, adding any other treatment to enzalutamide does not synergistically increase `objective_response` beyond the enzalutamide-only rate (~0.80).

analyses.code: fav subset; group by (other_tx, enz) and report mean response
analyses.result_summary: Among favorable enzalutamide-treated (~0.80 baseline), response with vs without each second drug: abiraterone 0.790 vs 0.801, docetaxel 0.801 vs 0.797, olaparib 0.773 vs 0.801, lu177-PSMA 0.809 vs 0.796, pembrolizumab 0.823 vs 0.797. All within sampling noise (no clear synergy or antagonism).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, the enzalutamide effect on `objective_response` is preserved across ECOG strata (i.e., strong benefit even at ECOG 2).

analyses.code: fav subset; stratify by ecog_ps and compare enz vs no-enz
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable: ECOG 0 enzalutamide 0.859 vs 0.213 (diff +0.646), ECOG 1 0.781 vs 0.159 (diff +0.622), ECOG 2 0.708 vs 0.119 (diff +0.590). All p<1e-150. Benefit is large at every ECOG level; baseline response declines with ECOG but treatment effect is mostly preserved.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within mcrpc=1 (CRPC) patients, no available treatment (enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab) improves `objective_response`.

analyses.code: mcrpc=1 subset; chi2 of each treatment vs response
analyses.result_summary: Within mCRPC (n=27,481), treated-vs-untreated differences: enzalutamide +0.008 (p=0.09), abiraterone +0.001 (p=0.89), docetaxel -0.001 (p=0.77), olaparib +0.002 (p=0.78), lu177-PSMA +0.005 (p=0.38), pembrolizumab +0.006 (p=0.59). No drug detectably improves response in CRPC in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for treatment and other covariates, the strongest baseline prognostic factors for `objective_response` (in order of effect strength) are mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, ecog_ps, brca2_mutation, weight_loss_pct_6mo, msi_high, albumin_g_dl, psa_ng_ml, and crp_mg_l - all in the predicted directions.

analyses.code: Multivariable logistic; sort by p-value; report ORs/coefs
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ORs: mcrpc 0.32, ar_v7_positive 0.50, ecog_ps 0.72/level, brca2_mutation 0.48, weight_loss 0.96/%, msi_high 0.65, albumin 1.14/g·dL, PSA 0.999/ng·mL, CRP 0.993/mg·L. All significant at p<1e-7. Lab/age/sex/Gleason/visceral_mets/PSMA-high/Na/K/Ca/creatinine/BUN/AST/ALT/bilirubin not significant. Confirms the prognostic ordering.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Two borderline subgroup signals - (a) `treatment_lu177_psma` lower response within visceral_mets=1, and (b) `treatment_pembrolizumab` lower response within visceral_mets=1 - do not survive multiple-testing scrutiny relative to the dozens of subgroup tests performed.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2: lu177 within visceral_mets=1; pembro within visceral_mets=1; logistic interaction tests
analyses.result_summary: lu177-PSMA in visceral_mets=1: -0.0243 (p=0.043, interaction OR 0.81 p=0.010). Pembrolizumab in visceral_mets=1: -0.0305 (p=0.135, interaction OR 0.77 p=0.047). Given roughly 60+ subgroup tests run for treatment heterogeneity, these are very weak (p > 0.001) and inconsistent (one drug shows it via stratification, the other via interaction). Treated as not robustly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL refined heterogeneity statement for `objective_response`: `treatment_enzalutamide` produces a large positive effect on `objective_response` (absolute increase ≈ +0.63) ONLY in patients defined by ALL of the following: `mcrpc` = 0 AND `brca2_mutation` = 0 AND `ar_v7_positive` = 0 AND `msi_high` = 0. Outside this joint subgroup the effect is essentially zero.

analyses.code: Joint subgroup chi2 plus complement chi2
analyses.result_summary: Joint favorable subgroup (n=15,681): enzalutamide 0.7978 vs 0.1721 (diff +0.6257, p≈0). Complement (n=34,319): enzalutamide 0.1234 vs 0.1547 (diff -0.031). Logistic interaction model with all four enz×marker terms shows the effect collapses when any one of mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high is positive (each interaction OR 0.11-0.21, all p<1e-26). Decision-tree CATE search recovers the same four-predicate rule. Subgroup definition is complete.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL heterogeneity statement for non-enzalutamide treatments on `objective_response`: none of `treatment_abiraterone`, `treatment_docetaxel`, `treatment_olaparib`, `treatment_lu177_psma`, or `treatment_pembrolizumab` has any reproducible subgroup of meaningful benefit on `objective_response` in this cohort - including in their canonical biomarker-defined subgroups (olaparib in BRCA2-mutated, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high) and including in the enzalutamide-favorable subgroup.

analyses.code: Across all stratified cells (treatment x marker x value) for the five non-enzalutamide drugs, plus logistic interaction terms
analyses.result_summary: Across ~60 stratified two-by-two cells for the five non-enzalutamide drugs, no cell has |diff|>0.04 with p<0.001. Canonical biomarker subgroups: olaparib in BRCA2+ -0.032 (p=0.058) - if anything trends worse; pembrolizumab in MSI-high +0.001 (p=1.0); lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high -0.001 (p=0.88). All multivariable adjusted main effects null (ORs 0.98-1.03, all p>0.30). No subgroup of benefit identified.
